'''''Link''''' was an [[MC80 Star Cruiser/Legends|MC80 Star Cruiser]] belonging to the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]].

==History==
When Imperial captain [[Namuura Din]] in the [[Victory-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Victory''-class Star Destroyer]] ''[[Zeplin]]'' defected to the Rebels, he told the ''Link''{{'s}} commander the truth about the [[Death Star II/Legends|Second Death Star]]. Namuura also gave them the ''[[Sealion]]'', a prototype of the [[Missile Boat]] [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]]. When [[Tan (title)|Tan]] [[Maarek Stele/Legends|Maarek Stele]] found out where the missile boat had gone, the ''Link'' entered [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]], while the ''Zeplin'' was captured.

However, the commander of the ''Link'', knowing the [[Imperial-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Imperial''-class Star Destroyer]] [[Garrett (Star Destroyer)|''Garrett'']] was in pursuit, made a desperate attempt to alert the [[Alliance Fleet/Legends|Rebel fleet]] at [[Sullust/Legends|Sullust]], which resulted in an engine failure due to overusing the engine's fuel rods, which forced a drop out of hyperspace. The ''Link'' was then stranded by the [[Immobilizer 418 Interdictor-class Heavy Cruiser|Immobilizer 418 cruiser]] ''[[Bew'a]]'', and with the help of Tan Maarek Stele, the ''Link''{{'s}} shields were depleted and her starfighter squadrons destroyed.

Rebel attempts to recover the ''Link'' through the [[CR92a Assassin-class corvette|''Assassin''-class corvette]] ''[[Crater]]'' and the [[Muurian transport]] ''[[Confal]]'' failed, as they were both destroyed by Stele. The ''Link'' was then captured and sent to [[Imperial Intelligence/Legends|Imperial Intelligence]] so concealed data could be extracted from its computer systems. What became of it afterwards is unknown, although it was noted that Imperial engineers would enjoy the close look at an enemy capital ship.
